# Notification Polling via WorkManager

**Issue:** [#67 — Push notification support](https://github.com/hackers-pub/android/issues/67)
**Date:** 2026-03-25

## Overview

Background notification polling using WorkManager's `PeriodicWorkRequest` (15-minute interval). Polls the existing GraphQL `Notifications` query, shows Android system notifications for new items, and wires up the unread dot on the bottom nav.

## Dependencies & Permissions

### New dependencies (`libs.versions.toml`)

- `androidx.work:work-runtime-ktx:2.10.0`
- `androidx.hilt:hilt-work:1.2.0`
- `androidx.hilt:hilt-compiler:1.2.0` (KSP processor)

### Manifest

- `<uses-permission android:name="android.permission.POST_NOTIFICATIONS" />`

## Architecture

```
┌─────────────────┐     writes      ┌──────────────────────┐    derives     ┌──────────────┐
│ NotificationWorker│ ──────────────→ │ NotificationStateManager│ ──────────→ │ hasUnread Flow│
│ (CoroutineWorker) │                │ (@Singleton)            │              └──────┬───────┘
└────────┬─────────┘                └───────────┬────────────┘                     │
         │                                      │                                  │ collect
         │ fetches                              │ markAsSeen()                     ▼
         ▼                                      │                          ┌──────────────┐
┌─────────────────┐                   ┌─────────┴──────────┐              │ AppViewModel  │
│ HackersPubRepo   │                   │NotificationsViewModel│              │ → BottomNav   │
│ .getNotifications│                   └────────────────────┘              └──────────────┘
└─────────────────┘
```

## Data Layer

### DataStore Keys (in `PreferencesManager`)

| Key | Type | Purpose |
|-----|------|---------|
| `LAST_SEEN_NOTIFICATION_ID` | `String` | ID of the newest notification the user has seen (updated when notifications screen opens) |
| `LAST_POLLED_NOTIFICATION_ID` | `String` | ID of the newest notification from the last background poll |

Two IDs instead of a boolean flag so the unread state can be accurately derived after app restart without re-fetching.

### `NotificationStateManager` (`data/local/NotificationStateManager.kt`)

- `@Singleton`, injected with `DataStore<Preferences>`
- `val hasUnread: StateFlow<Boolean>` — true when last-polled ID differs from last-seen ID
- `suspend fun updateLastPolledId(id: String)` — called by worker after fetching new notifications
- `suspend fun markAsSeen()` — called when user opens notifications screen; sets last-seen = last-polled
- `suspend fun clear()` — called on logout; resets both IDs

## NotificationWorker (`data/worker/NotificationWorker.kt`)

`@HiltWorker` extending `CoroutineWorker`. Injected with `HackersPubRepository`, `SessionManager`, `NotificationStateManager`.

### Execution flow

1. Check `sessionManager.isLoggedIn.first()` — return `Result.success()` if not logged in
2. Call `repository.getNotifications(refresh = true)` to fetch the latest page
3. Compare first notification ID against last polled ID
4. If different:
   - `notificationStateManager.updateLastPolledId(newId)`
   - Build and post Android system notifications for the new items
5. Return `Result.success()` on success, `Result.retry()` on network failure

### System notifications

- Channel ID: `"hackerspub_notifications"`, name: "Hackers' Pub"
- Group multiple notifications under a summary
- Each notification includes: actor name + action (e.g. "alice mentioned you")
- `PendingIntent` deep-links to the notifications screen
- Respects `POST_NOTIFICATIONS` permission — worker polls regardless, but only posts notifications if permission is granted

## Worker Registration & Lifecycle

### Scheduling

- **After login:** Enqueue `PeriodicWorkRequestBuilder<NotificationWorker>(15, TimeUnit.MINUTES)` with unique name `"notification_poll"`, policy `ExistingPeriodicWorkPolicy.KEEP`, constraint `NetworkType.CONNECTED`
- **On app launch (already logged in):** `AppViewModel.init` enqueues with `KEEP` policy (safe to call multiple times; does not restart the interval)

### Cancellation

- **On logout:** `WorkManager.cancelUniqueWork("notification_poll")` + `NotificationStateManager.clear()`

### WorkManager initialization

- `HackersPubApplication` implements `Configuration.Provider` to provide Hilt's `HiltWorkerFactory`

## UI Wiring

### `AppViewModel`

- Inject `NotificationStateManager`
- Expose `val hasUnread: StateFlow<Boolean>`
- Call `ensureNotificationPolling()` in `init` (enqueues work if logged in)

### `HackersPubApp.kt`

- Collect `appViewModel.hasUnread`
- Pass to notifications `BottomNavItem`: `hasNotificationDot = hasUnread`

### `NotificationsViewModel`

- Inject `NotificationStateManager`
- Call `markAsSeen()` when notifications load successfully

### POST_NOTIFICATIONS permission

- Request in `MainActivity` on Android 13+ via `rememberLauncherForActivityResult`
- Prompt after first successful login, not on cold start
- Worker polls regardless of permission; it just skips posting system notifications if denied

## New Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `data/local/NotificationStateManager.kt` | Singleton managing unread state via DataStore |
| `data/worker/NotificationWorker.kt` | Background polling worker |

## Modified Files

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `gradle/libs.versions.toml` | Add WorkManager + Hilt Work dependencies |
| `app/build.gradle.kts` | Add new dependency entries |
| `AndroidManifest.xml` | Add `POST_NOTIFICATIONS` permission |
| `HackersPubApplication.kt` | Implement `Configuration.Provider` for HiltWorkerFactory |
| `di/AppModule.kt` | Provide `NotificationStateManager`, `WorkManager` |
| `data/local/PreferencesManager.kt` | Add two new DataStore keys |
| `ui/AppViewModel.kt` | Expose `hasUnread`, enqueue polling on init |
| `ui/HackersPubApp.kt` | Wire `hasNotificationDot` from `hasUnread` state |
| `ui/screens/notifications/NotificationsViewModel.kt` | Call `markAsSeen()` on load |
| `MainActivity.kt` | Request `POST_NOTIFICATIONS` permission after login |

git add docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-25-notification-polling-design.md && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Address spec review feedback for notification polling design

- Clarify DataStore ownership (keys in NotificationStateManager, not PreferencesManager)
- Change hasUnread from StateFlow to cold Flow (consumers use stateIn)
- Add manifest entry to disable default WorkManagerInitializer
- Add NotificationManager.cancelAll() to logout flow
- Specify notification channel creation in Application.onCreate()
- Distinguish auth errors from network errors in worker retry logic
- Confirm markAsSeen() called on every refresh, not just initial load
- Add ProGuard/R8 verification note
- Fix AppModule changes (only @Provides for WorkManager)
EOF
)"

1→package pub.hackers.android.di
     2→
     3→import android.content.Context
     4→import androidx.datastore.core.DataStore
     5→import androidx.datastore.preferences.core.Preferences
     6→import androidx.datastore.preferences.preferencesDataStore
     7→import com.apollographql.apollo.ApolloClient
     8→import com.apollographql.apollo.cache.normalized.normalizedCache
     9→import com.apollographql.apollo.cache.normalized.sql.SqlNormalizedCacheFactory
    10→import com.apollographql.apollo.network.okHttpClient
    11→import dagger.Module
    12→import dagger.Provides
    13→import dagger.hilt.InstallIn
    14→import dagger.hilt.android.qualifiers.ApplicationContext
    15→import dagger.hilt.components.SingletonComponent
    16→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.first
    17→import kotlinx.coroutines.runBlocking
    18→import okhttp3.Interceptor
    19→import okhttp3.OkHttpClient
    20→import pub.hackers.android.data.local.SessionManager
    21→import javax.inject.Singleton
    22→
    23→private val Context.dataStore: DataStore<Preferences> by preferencesDataStore(name = "hackerspub_prefs")
    24→
    25→@Module
    26→@InstallIn(SingletonComponent::class)
    27→object AppModule {
    28→
    29→    @Provides
    30→    @Singleton
    31→    fun provideDataStore(@ApplicationContext context: Context): DataStore<Preferences> {
    32→        return context.dataStore
    33→    }
    34→
    35→    @Provides
    36→    @Singleton
    37→    fun provideSessionManager(dataStore: DataStore<Preferences>): SessionManager {
    38→        return SessionManager(dataStore)
    39→    }
    40→
    41→    @Provides
    42→    @Singleton
    43→    fun provideApolloClient(
    44→        @ApplicationContext context: Context,
    45→        sessionManager: SessionManager
    46→    ): ApolloClient {
    47→        val sqlNormalizedCacheFactory = SqlNormalizedCacheFactory(context, "apollo_cache.db")
    48→
    49→        val authInterceptor = Interceptor { chain ->
    50→            val token = runBlocking { sessionManager.sessionToken.first() }
    51→            val request = if (token != null) {
    52→                chain.request().newBuilder()
    53→                    .addHeader("Authorization", "Bearer $token")
    54→                    .build()
    55→            } else {
    56→                chain.request()
    57→            }
    58→            chain.proceed(request)
    59→        }
    60→
    61→        val okHttpClient = OkHttpClient.Builder()
    62→            .addInterceptor(authInterceptor)
    63→            .build()
    64→
    65→        return ApolloClient.Builder()
    66→            .serverUrl("https://hackers.pub/graphql")
    67→            .okHttpClient(okHttpClient)

     6→import androidx.datastore.preferences.core.booleanPreferencesKey

    10→import androidx.datastore.preferences.preferencesDataStore
    11→import dagger.hilt.android.qualifiers.ApplicationContext
    12→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.Flow
    13→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.map
    14→import javax.inject.Inject
    15→import javax.inject.Singleton
    16→
    17→private val Context.preferencesDataStore: DataStore<Preferences> by preferencesDataStore(name = "app_preferences")
    18→
    19→@Singleton
    20→class PreferencesManager @Inject constructor(
    21→    @ApplicationContext private val context: Context
    22→) {
    23→    companion object {
    24→        private val CONFIRM_BEFORE_DELETE = booleanPreferencesKey("confirm_before_delete")
    25→        private val CONFIRM_BEFORE_SHARE = booleanPreferencesKey("confirm_before_share")
    26→        private val TIMELINE_MAX_LENGTH = intPreferencesKey("timeline_max_length")
    27→        private val USE_IN_APP_BROWSER = booleanPreferencesKey("use_in_app_browser")
    28→        private val FONT_SIZE_MULTIPLIER = intPreferencesKey("font_size_multiplier") // stored as percentage (100 = 1.0x)
    29→        private val RECENT_SEARCHES = stringPreferencesKey("recent_searches")
    30→        private const val MAX_RECENT_SEARCHES = 10
    31→    }
    32→
    33→    val confirmBeforeDelete: Flow<Boolean> = context.preferencesDataStore.data.map { prefs ->
    34→        prefs[CONFIRM_BEFORE_DELETE] ?: true
    35→    }
    36→
    37→    val confirmBeforeShare: Flow<Boolean> = context.preferencesDataStore.data.map { prefs ->
    38→        prefs[CONFIRM_BEFORE_SHARE] ?: false
    39→    }
    40→
    41→    val timelineMaxLength: Flow<Int> = context.preferencesDataStore.data.map { prefs ->
    42→        prefs[TIMELINE_MAX_LENGTH] ?: 0 // 0 = unlimited
    43→    }
    44→
    45→    val useInAppBrowser: Flow<Boolean> = context.preferencesDataStore.data.map { prefs ->
    46→        prefs[USE_IN_APP_BROWSER] ?: true
    47→    }
    48→
    49→    suspend fun setUseInAppBrowser(value: Boolean) {
    50→        context.preferencesDataStore.edit { prefs ->
    51→            prefs[USE_IN_APP_BROWSER] = value
    52→        }
    53→    }
    54→
    55→    val fontSizePercent: Flow<Int> = context.preferencesDataStore.data.map { prefs ->
    56→        prefs[FONT_SIZE_MULTIPLIER] ?: 100
    57→    }
    58→
    59→    suspend fun setFontSizePercent(value: Int) {
    60→        context.preferencesDataStore.edit { prefs ->
    61→            prefs[FONT_SIZE_MULTIPLIER] = value.coerceIn(75, 200)
    62→        }
    63→    }
    64→
    65→    suspend fun setConfirmBeforeDelete(value: Boolean) {
    66→        context.preferencesDataStore.edit { prefs ->
    67→            prefs[CONFIRM_BEFORE_DELETE] = value
    68→        }
    69→    }
    70→
    71→    suspend fun setConfirmBeforeShare(value: Boolean) {
    72→        context.preferencesDataStore.edit { prefs ->
    73→            prefs[CONFIRM_BEFORE_SHARE] = value
    74→        }
    75→    }
    76→
    77→    suspend fun setTimelineMaxLength(value: Int) {
    78→        context.preferencesDataStore.edit { prefs ->
    79→            prefs[TIMELINE_MAX_LENGTH] = value
    80→        }
    81→    }
    82→
    83→    val recentSearches: Flow<List<String>> = context.preferencesDataStore.data.map { prefs ->
    84→        val raw = prefs[RECENT_SEARCHES] ?: ""
    85→        if (raw.isEmpty()) emptyList() else raw.split("\u0000")
    86→    }
    87→
    88→    suspend fun addRecentSearch(query: String) {
    89→        context.preferencesDataStore.edit { prefs ->
    90→            val raw = prefs[RECENT_SEARCHES] ?: ""
    91→            val existing = if (raw.isEmpty()) mutableListOf() else raw.split("\u0000").toMutableList()
    92→            existing.remove(query)
    93→            existing.add(0, query)
    94→            val trimmed = existing.take(MAX_RECENT_SEARCHES)
    95→            prefs[RECENT_SEARCHES] = trimmed.joinToString("\u0000")
    96→        }
    97→    }
    98→
    99→    suspend fun removeRecentSearch(query: String) {
   100→        context.preferencesDataStore.edit { prefs ->
   101→            val raw = prefs[RECENT_SEARCHES] ?: ""
   102→            val existing = if (raw.isEmpty()) mutableListOf() else raw.split("\u0000").toMutableList()
   103→            existing.remove(query)
   104→            prefs[RECENT_SEARCHES] = existing.joinToString("\u0000")
   105→        }
   106→    }
   107→
   108→    suspend fun clearRecentSearches() {
   109→        context.preferencesDataStore.edit { prefs ->
   110→            prefs[RECENT_SEARCHES] = ""
   111→        }
   112→    }
   113→}

    12→@Singleton
    13→class SessionManager @Inject constructor(

    15→) {

    17→        private val SESSION_TOKEN_KEY = stringPreferencesKey("session_token")
    18→        private val USERNAME_KEY = stringPreferencesKey("username")
    19→        private val USER_ID_KEY = stringPreferencesKey("user_id")
    20→        private val USER_HANDLE_KEY = stringPreferencesKey("user_handle")
    21→        private val USER_NAME_KEY = stringPreferencesKey("user_name")
    22→        private val USER_AVATAR_KEY = stringPreferencesKey("user_avatar")
    23→    }
    24→
    25→    val sessionToken: Flow<String?> = dataStore.data.map { preferences ->
    26→        preferences[SESSION_TOKEN_KEY]
    27→    }
    28→
    29→    val isLoggedIn: Flow<Boolean> = dataStore.data.map { preferences ->
    30→        preferences[SESSION_TOKEN_KEY] != null
    31→    }
    32→
    33→    val username: Flow<String?> = dataStore.data.map { preferences ->
    34→        preferences[USERNAME_KEY]
    35→    }
    36→
    37→    val userId: Flow<String?> = dataStore.data.map { preferences ->
    38→        preferences[USER_ID_KEY]
    39→    }
    40→
    41→    val userHandle: Flow<String?> = dataStore.data.map { preferences ->
    42→        preferences[USER_HANDLE_KEY]
    43→    }
    44→
    45→    val userName: Flow<String?> = dataStore.data.map { preferences ->
    46→        preferences[USER_NAME_KEY]
    47→    }
    48→
    49→    val userAvatar: Flow<String?> = dataStore.data.map { preferences ->
    50→        preferences[USER_AVATAR_KEY]
    51→    }
    52→
    53→    suspend fun saveSession(
    54→        token: String,
    55→        userId: String,
    56→        username: String,
    57→        handle: String,
    58→        name: String,
    59→        avatarUrl: String
    60→    ) {
    61→        dataStore.edit { preferences ->
    62→            preferences[SESSION_TOKEN_KEY] = token
    63→            preferences[USER_ID_KEY] = userId
    64→            preferences[USERNAME_KEY] = username
    65→            preferences[USER_HANDLE_KEY] = handle
    66→            preferences[USER_NAME_KEY] = name
    67→            preferences[USER_AVATAR_KEY] = avatarUrl
    68→        }

    74→            preferences.remove(USER_ID_KEY)

    76→            preferences.remove(USER_HANDLE_KEY)
    77→            preferences.remove(USER_NAME_KEY)
    78→            preferences.remove(USER_AVATAR_KEY)
    79→        }
    80→    }
    81→}

Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase and the design spec. Let me verify one more thing -- the `SessionManager` uses constructor injection (not `@Provides`), which is relevant for understanding the DI pattern consistency.

Let me now systematically verify each of the 9 previously-raised issues against the updated spec.

---

## Review Verdict: **APPROVED**

All nine issues from the previous review pass have been addressed in the updated spec. Here is the point-by-point verification:

### Issue 1: DataStore keys should be owned by NotificationStateManager, not PreferencesManager
**FIXED.** Line 56 explicitly states: "**DataStore keys** (owned by this class, not `PreferencesManager`)". The keys `LAST_SEEN_NOTIFICATION_ID` and `LAST_POLLED_NOTIFICATION_ID` are scoped to `NotificationStateManager`. This is consistent with the existing codebase where `PreferencesManager` (in `/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub-android/app/src/main/java/pub/hackers/android/data/local/PreferencesManager.kt`) owns its own keys and uses a separate DataStore (`"app_preferences"`), while `SessionManager` uses the shared `"hackerspub_prefs"` DataStore. The spec correctly places notification state in the session-scoped DataStore (line 54).

### Issue 2: NotificationStateManager uses constructor injection, only WorkManager needs @Provides
**FIXED.** Line 52-53 specifies `@Singleton` with `@Inject constructor`, injected with the Hilt-provided `DataStore<Preferences>`. The Modified Files table (line 153) correctly limits `AppModule` changes to adding only a `@Provides` for `WorkManager.getInstance(context)`. This is consistent with how `SessionManager` already works in the codebase -- it uses `@Inject constructor` but has an explicit `@Provides` in `AppModule` (line 37-39 of `AppModule.kt`). One note: `SessionManager` currently has a redundant `@Provides` despite having `@Inject constructor`; the spec avoids repeating that pattern for `NotificationStateManager`, which is correct.

### Issue 3: Must disable default WorkManagerInitializer in manifest
**FIXED.** Lines 21-32 provide the exact XML snippet for disabling the default `WorkManagerInitializer` via the `androidx-startup` provider with `tools:node="remove"`. Line 111 confirms `HackersPubApplication` implements `Configuration.Provider` to provide Hilt's `HiltWorkerFactory`. Line 112 cross-references back to the manifest section.

### Issue 4: Logout must cancel system notifications via NotificationManager.cancelAll()
**FIXED.** Line 107 explicitly includes `NotificationManager.cancelAll()` in the logout sequence, with the comment "dismiss any posted system notifications from the previous session". The full logout sequence (lines 104-107) is: cancel the work, clear state, dismiss notifications.

### Issue 5: hasUnread should be Flow<Boolean> not StateFlow<Boolean>
**FIXED.** Line 67 declares `val hasUnread: Flow<Boolean>` as a cold Flow derived from DataStore. Line 67 also explains: "Consumers (e.g. `AppViewModel`) convert to `StateFlow` via `stateIn` in their own scope." Line 119 in the AppViewModel section shows the exact `stateIn` call with `SharingStarted.WhileSubscribed(5000)`. This is the correct layered approach -- the state manager exposes a cold Flow, and each consumer scopes it to their own lifecycle.

### Issue 6: Specify where NotificationChannel is created
**FIXED.** Line 89 explicitly states: "Notification channel created in `HackersPubApplication.onCreate()`". Line 152 in the Modified Files table confirms `HackersPubApplication.kt` will be modified to create the notification channel. This is the standard Android pattern since the channel must exist before any notification is posted, and `Application.onCreate()` is the earliest reliable point.

### Issue 7: Distinguish auth errors from network errors in worker retry
**FIXED.** Line 80 spells out the error-handling strategy: "distinguish auth errors (return `Result.success()`, not worth retrying) from network errors (return `Result.retry()` with default exponential backoff starting at 10s)". This prevents the worker from pointlessly retrying when the token is invalid/expired while still recovering from transient network failures.

### Issue 8: Confirm markAsSeen() on every refresh
**FIXED.** Line 69 states `markAsSeen()` is "called on every successful notifications load/refresh". Line 156 in the Modified Files table confirms: "Call `markAsSeen()` on every load/refresh". Line 162 even addresses the race condition where the worker fires while the user is on the notifications screen, noting that `markAsSeen()` on every refresh handles this gracefully.

### Issue 9: ProGuard note needed
**FIXED.** Lines 161 provides the ProGuard/R8 guidance: "Verify that `hilt-work`'s bundled consumer rules are sufficient for `@HiltWorker` with `isMinifyEnabled = true`. Add explicit keep rules for `NotificationWorker` if needed." This is appropriate given that `isMinifyEnabled = true` in the release build type (line 38 of `app/build.gradle.kts`).

### Additional observations (no action required)

The spec is well-structured and internally consistent. A few things I noted positively:

- The architecture diagram (lines 36-47) clearly shows data flow direction.
- The two-ID approach (last-seen vs. last-polled, lines 62-63) is a sound design that avoids re-fetching on restart.
- The `KEEP` policy for `ExistingPeriodicWorkPolicy` (line 99-100) is correct -- it prevents restarting the interval on every app launch.
- The new files table (lines 140-143) and modified files table (lines 146-158) provide a clear implementation roadmap.
- The future note about WebPush/UnifiedPush (line 163) shows forward-thinking design without over-engineering the current solution.

**Relevant file:** `/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub-android/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-25-notification-polling-design.md`

Add explicit keep rules for `NotificationWorker` if needed." This is appropriate given that `isMinifyEnabled = true` in the release build type (line 38 of `app/build.gradle.kts`).

### Additional observations (no action required)

The spec is well-structured and internally consistent. A few things I noted positively:

- The architecture diagram (lines 36-47) clearly shows data flow direction.
- The two-ID approach (last-seen vs. last-polled, lines 62-63) is a sound design that avoids re-fetching on restart.
- The `KEEP` policy for `ExistingPeriodicWorkPolicy` (line 99-100) is correct -- it prevents restarting the interval on every app launch.
- The new files table (lines 140-143) and modified files table (lines 146-158) provide a clear implementation roadmap.
- The future note about WebPush/UnifiedPush (line 163) shows forward-thinking design without over-engineering the current solution.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/kodingwarrior/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/5.0.5/skills/writing-plans

# Writing Plans

## Overview

Write comprehensive implementation plans assuming the engineer has zero context for our codebase and questionable taste. Document everything they need to know: which files to touch for each task, code, testing, docs they might need to check, how to test it. Give them the whole plan as bite-sized tasks. DRY. YAGNI. TDD. Frequent commits.

Assume they are a skilled developer, but know almost nothing about our toolset or problem domain. Assume they don't know good test design very well.

**Announce at start:** "I'm using the writing-plans skill to create the implementation plan."

**Context:** This should be run in a dedicated worktree (created by brainstorming skill).

**Save plans to:** `docs/superpowers/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<feature-name>.md`
- (User preferences for plan location override this default)

## Scope Check

If the spec covers multiple independent subsystems, it should have been broken into sub-project specs during brainstorming. If it wasn't, suggest breaking this into separate plans — one per subsystem. Each plan should produce working, testable software on its own.

## File Structure

Before defining tasks, map out which files will be created or modified and what each one is responsible for. This is where decomposition decisions get locked in.

- Design units with clear boundaries and well-defined interfaces. Each file should have one clear responsibility.
- You reason best about code you can hold in context at once, and your edits are more reliable when files are focused. Prefer smaller, focused files over large ones that do too much.
- Files that change together should live together. Split by responsibility, not by technical layer.
- In existing codebases, follow established patterns. If the codebase uses large files, don't unilaterally restructure - but if a file you're modifying has grown unwieldy, including a split in the plan is reasonable.

This structure informs the task decomposition. Each task should produce self-contained changes that make sense independently.

## Bite-Sized Task Granularity

**Each step is one action (2-5 minutes):**
- "Write the failing test" - step
- "Run it to make sure it fails" - step
- "Implement the minimal code to make the test pass" - step
- "Run the tests and make sure they pass" - step
- "Commit" - step

## Plan Document Header

**Every plan MUST start with this header:**

```markdown
# [Feature Name] Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** [One sentence describing what this builds]

**Architecture:** [2-3 sentences about approach]

**Tech Stack:** [Key technologies/libraries]

---
```

## Task Structure

````markdown
### Task N: [Component Name]

**Files:**
- Create: `exact/path/to/file.py`
- Modify: `exact/path/to/existing.py:123-145`
- Test: `tests/exact/path/to/test.py`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing test**

```python
def test_specific_behavior():
    result = function(input)
    assert result == expected
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run test to verify it fails**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_name -v`
Expected: FAIL with "function not defined"

- [ ] **Step 3: Write minimal implementation**

```python
def function(input):
    return expected
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Run test to verify it passes**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_name -v`
Expected: PASS

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add tests/path/test.py src/path/file.py
git commit -m "feat: add specific feature"
```
````

## Remember
- Exact file paths always
- Complete code in plan (not "add validation")
- Exact commands with expected output
- Reference relevant skills with @ syntax
- DRY, YAGNI, TDD, frequent commits

## Plan Review Loop

After writing the complete plan:

1. Dispatch a single plan-document-reviewer subagent (see plan-document-reviewer-prompt.md) with precisely crafted review context — never your session history. This keeps the reviewer focused on the plan, not your thought process.
   - Provide: path to the plan document, path to spec document
2. If ❌ Issues Found: fix the issues, re-dispatch reviewer for the whole plan
3. If ✅ Approved: proceed to execution handoff

**Review loop guidance:**
- Same agent that wrote the plan fixes it (preserves context)
- If loop exceeds 3 iterations, surface to human for guidance
- Reviewers are advisory — explain disagreements if you believe feedback is incorrect

## Execution Handoff

After saving the plan, offer execution choice:

**"Plan complete and saved to `docs/superpowers/plans/<filename>.md`. Two execution options:**

**1. Subagent-Driven (recommended)** - I dispatch a fresh subagent per task, review between tasks, fast iteration

**2. Inline Execution** - Execute tasks in this session using executing-plans, batch execution with checkpoints

**Which approach?"**

**If Subagent-Driven chosen:**
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development
- Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review

**If Inline Execution chosen:**
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** Use superpowers:executing-plans
- Batch execution with checkpoints for review

   108→    data object Profile : DetailScreen("profile/{handle}") {
   109→        fun createRoute(handle: String) = "profile/$handle"
   110→    }
   111→}
   112→
   113→@Composable
   114→fun HackersPubApp(
   115→    deepLinkData: pub.hackers.android.DeepLinkData? = null,
   116→    viewModel: AppViewModel = hiltViewModel()
   117→) {
   118→    val navController = rememberNavController()
   119→    val isLoggedIn by viewModel.isLoggedIn.collectAsState(initial = false)
   120→
   121→    val fontSizePercent by viewModel.preferencesManager.fontSizePercent.collectAsState(initial = 100)
   122→
   123→    // Handle deep link for verification
   124→    LaunchedEffect(deepLinkData) {
   125→        deepLinkData?.let {
   126→            navController.navigate("signin?token=${it.token}&code=${it.code}")
   127→        }
   128→    }
   129→
   130→    ProvideInAppBrowserUriHandler(preferencesManager = viewModel.preferencesManager) {
   131→    CompositionLocalProvider(LocalFontScale provides (fontSizePercent / 100f)) {
   132→
   133→    val bottomNavItems = if (isLoggedIn) {
   134→        listOf(
   135→            BottomNavItem(
   136→                route = Screen.Timeline.route,
   137→                label = stringResource(R.string.nav_timeline),
   138→                icon = Icons.Outlined.Home,
   139→                selectedIcon = Icons.Filled.Home,
   140→            ),
   141→            BottomNavItem(
   142→                route = Screen.Explore.route,
   143→                label = stringResource(R.string.nav_explore),
   144→                icon = Icons.Outlined.Explore,
   145→                selectedIcon = Icons.Filled.Explore,
   146→            ),
   147→            BottomNavItem(
   148→                route = Screen.Notifications.route,
   149→                label = stringResource(R.string.nav_notifications),
   150→                icon = Icons.Outlined.Notifications,
   151→                selectedIcon = Icons.Filled.Notifications,
   152→                hasNotificationDot = false, // TODO: wire up unread notification state
   153→            ),
   154→            BottomNavItem(
   155→                route = Screen.Search.route,
   156→                label = stringResource(R.string.nav_search),
   157→                icon = Icons.Outlined.Search,
   158→                selectedIcon = Icons.Filled.Search,
   159→            ),
   160→        )
   161→    } else {
   162→        listOf(
   163→            BottomNavItem(
   164→                route = Screen.Explore.route,
   165→                label = stringResource(R.string.nav_explore),
   166→                icon = Icons.Outlined.Explore,
   167→                selectedIcon = Icons.Filled.Explore,
   168→            ),
   169→            BottomNavItem(
   170→                route = Screen.Search.route,
   171→                label = stringResource(R.string.nav_search),
   172→                icon = Icons.Outlined.Search,
   173→                selectedIcon = Icons.Filled.Search,
   174→            ),
   175→            BottomNavItem(
   176→                route = Screen.Settings.route,
   177→                label = stringResource(R.string.nav_settings),
   178→                icon = Icons.Outlined.Settings,
   179→                selectedIcon = Icons.Filled.Settings,
   180→            ),
   181→        )
   182→    }
   183→
   184→    val navBackStackEntry by navController.currentBackStackEntryAsState()
   185→    val currentDestination = navBackStackEntry?.destination
   186→    val currentRoute = currentDestination?.route
   187→    val showBottomBar = bottomNavItems.any { it.route == currentRoute }
   188→
   189→    Scaffold(
   190→        bottomBar = {
   191→            if (showBottomBar) {
   192→                BottomNavBar(
   193→                    items = bottomNavItems,
   194→                    selectedRoute = currentRoute ?: "",
   195→                    onItemSelected = { item ->
   196→                        navController.navigate(item.route) {
   197→                            popUpTo(navController.graph.findStartDestination().id) {
   198→                                saveState = true
   199→                            }
   200→                            launchSingleTop = true
   201→                            restoreState = true
   202→                        }
   203→                    },
   204→                )
   205→            }
   206→        }
   207→    ) { innerPadding ->
   208→        NavHost(
   209→            navController = navController,
   210→            startDestination = if (isLoggedIn) Screen.Timeline.route else Screen.Explore.route,
   211→            modifier = Modifier.padding(innerPadding)
   212→        ) {
   213→            composable(Screen.Timeline.route) {
   214→                TimelineScreen(
   215→                    onPostClick = { postId ->
   216→                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.PostDetail.createRoute(postId))
   217→                    },
   218→                    onProfileClick = { handle ->
   219→                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Profile.createRoute(handle))
   220→                    },
   221→                    onComposeClick = { replyTo ->
   222→                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Compose.createRoute(replyTo))
   223→                    },
   224→                    onQuoteClick = { postId ->
   225→                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Compose.createRoute(quoteOf = postId))
   226→                    },
   227→                    onSettingsClick = {
   228→                        navController.navigate(Screen.Settings.route)
   229→                    }
   230→                )
   231→            }
   232→
   233→            composable(Screen.Notifications.route) {
   234→                NotificationsScreen(
   235→                    onPostClick = { postId ->
   236→                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.PostDetail.createRoute(postId))
   237→                    },
   238→                    onProfileClick = { handle ->
   239→                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Profile.createRoute(handle))
   240→                    }
   241→                )
   242→            }
   243→
   244→            composable(Screen.Explore.route) {
   245→                ExploreScreen(
   246→                    onPostClick = { postId ->
   247→                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.PostDetail.createRoute(postId))
   248→                    },
   249→                    onProfileClick = { handle ->
   250→                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Profile.createRoute(handle))
   251→                    },
   252→                    onReplyClick = { postId ->
   253→                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Compose.createRoute(postId))
   254→                    },
   255→                    onQuoteClick = { postId ->
   256→                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Compose.createRoute(quoteOf = postId))
   257→                    },
   258→                    onSignInClick = {
   259→                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.SignIn.createRoute())
   260→                    },
   261→                    isLoggedIn = isLoggedIn
   262→                )
   263→            }
   264→
   265→            composable(Screen.Search.route) {
   266→                SearchScreen(
   267→                    onPostClick = { postId ->
   268→                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.PostDetail.createRoute(postId))
   269→                    },
   270→                    onProfileClick = { handle ->
   271→                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Profile.createRoute(handle))
   272→                    },
   273→                    onReplyClick = { postId ->
   274→                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Compose.createRoute(replyTo = postId))
   275→                    },
   276→                    onQuoteClick = { postId ->
   277→                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Compose.createRoute(quoteOf = postId))
   278→                    }
   279→                )
   280→            }
   281→
   282→            composable(Screen.Settings.route) {
   283→                SettingsScreen(
   284→                    onSignInClick = {
   285→                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.SignIn.createRoute())
   286→                    },
   287→                    onSignOutComplete = {
   288→                        navController.navigate(Screen.Explore.route) {
   289→                            popUpTo(0) { inclusive = true }
   290→                        }
   291→                    },
   292→                    isLoggedIn = isLoggedIn
   293→                )
   294→            }
   295→
   296→            composable(
   297→                route = DetailScreen.SignIn.route,
   298→                arguments = listOf(
   299→                    navArgument("token") {
   300→                        type = NavType.StringType
   301→                        nullable = true
   302→                        defaultValue = null
   303→                    },
   304→                    navArgument("code") {
   305→                        type = NavType.StringType
   306→                        nullable = true
   307→                        defaultValue = null
   308→                    }
   309→                )
   310→            ) { backStackEntry ->
   311→                val token = backStackEntry.arguments?.getString("token")
   312→                val code = backStackEntry.arguments?.getString("code")
   313→                SignInScreen(
   314→                    deepLinkToken = token,
   315→                    deepLinkCode = code,
   316→                    onSignInSuccess = {
   317→                        navController.navigate(Screen.Timeline.route) {
   318→                            popUpTo(0) { inclusive = true }
   319→                        }
   320→                    },
   321→                    onNavigateBack = {
   322→                        navController.popBackStack()
   323→                    }
   324→                )
   325→            }
   326→
   327→            composable(
   328→                route = DetailScreen.Compose.route,
   329→                arguments = listOf(
   330→                    navArgument("replyTo") {
   331→                        type = NavType.StringType
   332→                        nullable = true
   333→                        defaultValue = null
   334→                    },
   335→                    navArgument("quoteOf") {
   336→                        type = NavType.StringType
   337→                        nullable = true
   338→                        defaultValue = null
   339→                    }
   340→                )
   341→            ) { backStackEntry ->
   342→                val replyTo = backStackEntry.arguments?.getString("replyTo")
   343→                val quoteOf = backStackEntry.arguments?.getString("quoteOf")
   344→                ComposeScreen(
   345→                    replyToId = replyTo,
   346→                    quotedPostId = quoteOf,
   347→                    onPostSuccess = {
   348→                        navController.popBackStack()
   349→                    },
   350→                    onNavigateBack = {
   351→                        navController.popBackStack()
   352→                    }
   353→                )
   354→            }
   355→
   356→            composable(
   357→                route = DetailScreen.PostDetail.route,
   358→                arguments = listOf(navArgument("postId") { type = NavType.StringType })
   359→            ) { backStackEntry ->
   360→                val postId = backStackEntry.arguments?.getString("postId") ?: return@composable
   361→                PostDetailScreen(
   362→                    postId = postId,
   363→                    onNavigateBack = {
   364→                        navController.popBackStack()
   365→                    },
   366→                    onProfileClick = { handle ->
   367→                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Profile.createRoute(handle))
   368→                    },
   369→                    onReplyClick = { id ->
   370→                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Compose.createRoute(replyTo = id))
   371→                    },
   372→                    onQuoteClick = { id ->
   373→                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Compose.createRoute(quoteOf = id))
   374→                    },
   375→                    onPostClick = { id ->
   376→                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.PostDetail.createRoute(id))
   377→                    },
   378→                    isLoggedIn = isLoggedIn
   379→                )
   380→            }
   381→
   382→            composable(
   383→                route = DetailScreen.Profile.route,
   384→                arguments = listOf(navArgument("handle") { type = NavType.StringType })
   385→            ) { backStackEntry ->
   386→                val handle = backStackEntry.arguments?.getString("handle") ?: return@composable
   387→                ProfileScreen(
   388→                    handle = handle,
   389→                    onNavigateBack = {
   390→                        navController.popBackStack()
   391→                    },
   392→                    onPostClick = { postId ->
   393→                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.PostDetail.createRoute(postId))
   394→                    },
   395→                    onProfileClick = { profileHandle ->
   396→                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Profile.createRoute(profileHandle))
   397→                    },
   398→                    onReplyClick = { postId ->
   399→                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Compose.createRoute(replyTo = postId))
   400→                    },
   401→                    onQuoteClick = { postId ->
   402→                        navController.navigate(DetailScreen.Compose.createRoute(quoteOf = postId))
   403→                    }
   404→                )
   405→            }
   406→        }
   407→    }
   408→    } // CompositionLocalProvider
   409→    } // ProvideInAppBrowserUriHandler
   410→}

1→package pub.hackers.android.data.local
     2→
     3→import androidx.datastore.core.DataStore
     4→import androidx.datastore.preferences.core.Preferences
     5→import androidx.datastore.preferences.core.edit
     6→import androidx.datastore.preferences.core.stringPreferencesKey
     7→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.Flow
     8→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.map
     9→import javax.inject.Inject
    10→import javax.inject.Singleton
    11→
    12→@Singleton
    13→class SessionManager @Inject constructor(
    14→    private val dataStore: DataStore<Preferences>
    15→) {
    16→    companion object {
    17→        private val SESSION_TOKEN_KEY = stringPreferencesKey("session_token")
    18→        private val USERNAME_KEY = stringPreferencesKey("username")
    19→        private val USER_ID_KEY = stringPreferencesKey("user_id")
    20→        private val USER_HANDLE_KEY = stringPreferencesKey("user_handle")
    21→        private val USER_NAME_KEY = stringPreferencesKey("user_name")
    22→        private val USER_AVATAR_KEY = stringPreferencesKey("user_avatar")
    23→    }
    24→
    25→    val sessionToken: Flow<String?> = dataStore.data.map { preferences ->
    26→        preferences[SESSION_TOKEN_KEY]
    27→    }
    28→
    29→    val isLoggedIn: Flow<Boolean> = dataStore.data.map { preferences ->
    30→        preferences[SESSION_TOKEN_KEY] != null
    31→    }
    32→
    33→    val username: Flow<String?> = dataStore.data.map { preferences ->
    34→        preferences[USERNAME_KEY]
    35→    }
    36→
    37→    val userId: Flow<String?> = dataStore.data.map { preferences ->
    38→        preferences[USER_ID_KEY]
    39→    }
    40→
    41→    val userHandle: Flow<String?> = dataStore.data.map { preferences ->
    42→        preferences[USER_HANDLE_KEY]
    43→    }
    44→
    45→    val userName: Flow<String?> = dataStore.data.map { preferences ->
    46→        preferences[USER_NAME_KEY]
    47→    }
    48→
    49→    val userAvatar: Flow<String?> = dataStore.data.map { preferences ->
    50→        preferences[USER_AVATAR_KEY]
    51→    }
    52→
    53→    suspend fun saveSession(
    54→        token: String,
    55→        userId: String,
    56→        username: String,
    57→        handle: String,
    58→        name: String,
    59→        avatarUrl: String
    60→    ) {
    61→        dataStore.edit { preferences ->
    62→            preferences[SESSION_TOKEN_KEY] = token
    63→            preferences[USER_ID_KEY] = userId
    64→            preferences[USERNAME_KEY] = username
    65→            preferences[USER_HANDLE_KEY] = handle
    66→            preferences[USER_NAME_KEY] = name
    67→            preferences[USER_AVATAR_KEY] = avatarUrl
    68→        }
    69→    }
    70→
    71→    suspend fun clearSession() {
    72→        dataStore.edit { preferences ->
    73→            preferences.remove(SESSION_TOKEN_KEY)
    74→            preferences.remove(USER_ID_KEY)
    75→            preferences.remove(USERNAME_KEY)
    76→            preferences.remove(USER_HANDLE_KEY)
    77→            preferences.remove(USER_NAME_KEY)
    78→            preferences.remove(USER_AVATAR_KEY)
    79→        }
    80→    }
    81→}
    82→

1→package pub.hackers.android
     2→
     3→import android.content.Intent
     4→import android.os.Bundle
     5→import androidx.activity.ComponentActivity
     6→import androidx.activity.compose.setContent
     7→import androidx.activity.enableEdgeToEdge
     8→import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.fillMaxSize
     9→import androidx.compose.material3.Surface
    10→import androidx.compose.runtime.getValue
    11→import androidx.compose.runtime.mutableStateOf
    12→import androidx.compose.runtime.setValue
    13→import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
    14→import dagger.hilt.android.AndroidEntryPoint
    15→import pub.hackers.android.ui.HackersPubApp
    16→import pub.hackers.android.ui.theme.HackersPubTheme
    17→import pub.hackers.android.ui.theme.LocalAppColors
    18→
    19→data class DeepLinkData(
    20→    val token: String,
    21→    val code: String
    22→)
    23→
    24→@AndroidEntryPoint
    25→class MainActivity : ComponentActivity() {
    26→
    27→    private var deepLinkData by mutableStateOf<DeepLinkData?>(null)
    28→
    29→    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
    30→        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
    31→        handleDeepLink(intent)
    32→        enableEdgeToEdge()
    33→        setContent {
    34→            HackersPubTheme {
    35→                Surface(
    36→                    modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize(),
    37→                    color = LocalAppColors.current.background
    38→                ) {
    39→                    HackersPubApp(deepLinkData = deepLinkData)
    40→                }
    41→            }
    42→        }
    43→    }
    44→
    45→    override fun onNewIntent(intent: Intent) {
    46→        super.onNewIntent(intent)
    47→        handleDeepLink(intent)
    48→    }
    49→
    50→    private fun handleDeepLink(intent: Intent?) {
    51→        val data = intent?.data ?: return
    52→        if (data.scheme == "hackerspub" && data.host == "verify") {
    53→            val token = data.getQueryParameter("token")
    54→            val code = data.getQueryParameter("code")
    55→            if (token != null && code != null) {
    56→                deepLinkData = DeepLinkData(token = token, code = code)
    57→            }
    58→        }
    59→    }
    60→}
    61→

    44→@Singleton
    45→class HackersPubRepository @Inject constructor(
    46→    private val apolloClient: ApolloClient
    47→) {
    48→    suspend fun getPublicTimeline(after: String? = null, refresh: Boolean = false): Result<TimelineResult> {
    49→        return try {
    50→            val response = apolloClient.query(PublicTimelineQuery(Optional.presentIfNotNull(after)))
    51→                .apply { if (refresh) fetchPolicy(FetchPolicy.NetworkOnly) }
    52→                .execute()
    53→
    54→            if (response.hasErrors()) {
    55→                Result.failure(Exception(response.errors?.firstOrNull()?.message ?: "Unknown error"))
    56→            } else {
    57→                val data = response.data?.publicTimeline
    58→                Result.success(
    59→                    TimelineResult(
    60→                        posts = data?.edges?.mapNotNull { edge ->
    61→                            edge.node.postFields.toPost(edge.node.sharedPost?.sharedPostFields?.toPost())
    62→                        } ?: emptyList(),
    63→                        hasNextPage = data?.pageInfo?.hasNextPage ?: false,
    64→                        endCursor = data?.pageInfo?.endCursor
    65→                    )
    66→                )
    67→            }
    68→        } catch (e: Exception) {
    69→            Result.failure(e)
    70→        }
    71→    }
    72→
    73→    suspend fun getLocalTimeline(after: String? = null, refresh: Boolean = false): Result<TimelineResult> {
    74→        return try {
    75→            val response = apolloClient.query(LocalTimelineQuery(Optional.presentIfNotNull(after)))
    76→                .apply { if (refresh) fetchPolicy(FetchPolicy.NetworkOnly) }
    77→                .execute()
    78→
    79→            if (response.hasErrors()) {
    80→                Result.failure(Exception(response.errors?.firstOrNull()?.message ?: "Unknown error"))
    81→            } else {
    82→                val data = response.data?.publicTimeline
    83→                Result.success(
    84→                    TimelineResult(
    85→                        posts = data?.edges?.mapNotNull { edge ->
    86→                            edge.node.postFields.toPost(edge.node.sharedPost?.sharedPostFields?.toPost())
    87→                        } ?: emptyList(),
    88→                        hasNextPage = data?.pageInfo?.hasNextPage ?: false,
    89→                        endCursor = data?.pageInfo?.endCursor
    90→                    )
    91→                )
    92→            }

    97→
    98→    suspend fun getPersonalTimeline(after: String? = null, refresh: Boolean = false): Result<TimelineResult> {
    99→        return try {
   100→            val response = apolloClient.query(PersonalTimelineQuery(Optional.presentIfNotNull(after)))
   101→                .apply { if (refresh) fetchPolicy(FetchPolicy.NetworkOnly) }
   102→                .execute()
   103→
   104→            if (response.hasErrors()) {
   105→                Result.failure(Exception(response.errors?.firstOrNull()?.message ?: "Unknown error"))
   106→            } else {
   107→                val data = response.data?.personalTimeline
   108→                Result.success(
   109→                    TimelineResult(
   110→                        posts = data?.edges?.mapNotNull { edge ->
   111→                            edge.node.postFields.toPost(edge.node.sharedPost?.sharedPostFields?.toPost())
   112→                        } ?: emptyList(),

   122→
   123→    suspend fun getNotifications(after: String? = null, refresh: Boolean = false): Result<NotificationsResult> {
   124→        return try {
   125→            val response = apolloClient.query(NotificationsQuery(Optional.presentIfNotNull(after)))
   126→                .apply { if (refresh) fetchPolicy(FetchPolicy.NetworkOnly) }
   127→                .execute()
   128→
   129→            if (response.hasErrors()) {
   130→                Result.failure(Exception(response.errors?.firstOrNull()?.message ?: "Unknown error"))
   131→            } else {
   132→                val data = response.data?.viewer?.notifications
   133→                Result.success(
   134→                    NotificationsResult(
   135→                        notifications = data?.edges?.mapNotNull { edge ->
   136→                            edge.node.toNotification()
   137→                        } ?: emptyList(),
   138→                        hasNextPage = data?.pageInfo?.hasNextPage ?: false,
   139→                        endCursor = data?.pageInfo?.endCursor

   148→    suspend fun searchPosts(query: String): Result<List<Post>> {
   149→        return try {
   150→            val response = apolloClient.query(SearchPostQuery(query)).execute()

   165→    suspend fun getPostDetail(id: String, repliesAfter: String? = null): Result<PostDetailResult> {
   166→        return try {
   167→            val response = apolloClient.query(
   168→                PostDetailQuery(id, Optional.presentIfNotNull(repliesAfter))
   169→            ).execute()
   170→
   171→            if (response.hasErrors()) {
   172→                Result.failure(Exception(response.errors?.firstOrNull()?.message ?: "Unknown error"))
   173→            } else {
   174→                val node = response.data?.node?.onPost
   175→                    ?: return Result.failure(Exception("Post not found"))
   176→
   177→                val post = node.postFields.toPost(
   178→                    sharedPost = node.sharedPost?.sharedPostFields?.toPost(),
   179→                    replyTarget = node.replyTarget?.postFields?.toPost(),
   180→                    visibility = node.visibility.toPostVisibility()
   181→                )
   182→
   183→                val reactionGroups = node.reactionGroups.mapNotNull { group ->
   184→                    when {
   185→                        group.onEmojiReactionGroup != null -> ReactionGroup(
   186→                            emoji = group.onEmojiReactionGroup.emoji,
   187→                            customEmoji = null,
   188→                            count = group.onEmojiReactionGroup.reactors.totalCount,
   189→                            reactors = group.onEmojiReactionGroup.reactors.edges.map {
   190→                                it.node.actorFields.toActor()
   191→                            },
   192→                            viewerHasReacted = group.onEmojiReactionGroup.reactors.viewerHasReacted
   193→                        )
   194→                        group.onCustomEmojiReactionGroup != null -> ReactionGroup(
   195→                            emoji = null,
   196→                            customEmoji = CustomEmoji(
   197→                                id = group.onCustomEmojiReactionGroup.customEmoji.id,
   198→                                name = group.onCustomEmojiReactionGroup.customEmoji.name,
   199→                                imageUrl = group.onCustomEmojiReactionGroup.customEmoji.imageUrl
   200→                            ),
   201→                            count = group.onCustomEmojiReactionGroup.reactors.totalCount,
   202→                            reactors = group.onCustomEmojiReactionGroup.reactors.edges.map {
   203→                                it.node.actorFields.toActor()
   204→                            },
   205→                            viewerHasReacted = group.onCustomEmojiReactionGroup.reactors.viewerHasReacted
   206→                        )
   207→                        else -> null
   208→                    }
   209→                }
   210→
   211→                val replies = node.replies.edges.mapNotNull { edge ->
   212→                    edge.node.postFields.toPost(edge.node.sharedPost?.sharedPostFields?.toPost())
   213→                }
   214→
   215→                Result.success(
   216→                    PostDetailResult(
   217→                        post = post,
   218→                        reactionGroups = reactionGroups,
   219→                        replies = replies,
   220→                        hasMoreReplies = node.replies.pageInfo.hasNextPage,
   221→                        repliesEndCursor = node.replies.pageInfo.endCursor
   222→                    )
   223→                )
   224→            }
   225→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   226→            Result.failure(e)
   227→        }
   228→    }

   355→    suspend fun createNote(
   356→        content: String,
   357→        language: String = "en",
   358→        visibility: PostVisibility = PostVisibility.PUBLIC,
   359→        replyTargetId: String? = null,
   360→        quotedPostId: String? = null
   361→    ): Result<Post> {
   362→        return try {
   363→            val gqlVisibility = when (visibility) {
   364→                PostVisibility.PUBLIC -> GqlPostVisibility.PUBLIC
   365→                PostVisibility.UNLISTED -> GqlPostVisibility.UNLISTED
   366→                PostVisibility.FOLLOWERS -> GqlPostVisibility.FOLLOWERS
   367→                PostVisibility.DIRECT -> GqlPostVisibility.DIRECT
   368→                PostVisibility.NONE -> GqlPostVisibility.NONE
   369→            }
   370→
   371→            val response = apolloClient.mutation(
   372→                CreateNoteMutation(
   373→                    content = content,
   374→                    language = language,
   375→                    visibility = gqlVisibility,
   376→                    replyTargetId = Optional.presentIfNotNull(replyTargetId),
   377→                    quotedPostId = Optional.presentIfNotNull(quotedPostId)
   378→                )
   379→            ).execute()
   380→
   381→            if (response.hasErrors()) {
   382→                Result.failure(Exception(response.errors?.firstOrNull()?.message ?: "Unknown error"))
   383→            } else {
   384→                val result = response.data?.createNote
   385→                when {
   386→                    result?.onCreateNotePayload != null -> {
   387→                        val note = result.onCreateNotePayload.note
   388→                        Result.success(
   389→                            Post(
   390→                                id = note.id,
   391→                                typename = "Note",
   392→                                name = null,
   393→                                published = Instant.parse(note.published.toString()),
   394→                                summary = null,
   395→                                content = note.content.toString(),
   396→                                excerpt = "",
   397→                                url = null,
   398→                                viewerHasShared = false,
   399→                                actor = Actor("", null, "", ""),
   400→                                media = emptyList(),
   401→                                engagementStats = EngagementStats(0, 0, 0, 0),
   402→                                mentions = emptyList()
   403→                            )
   404→                        )
   405→                    }
   406→                    result?.onInvalidInputError != null -> {
   407→                        Result.failure(Exception("Invalid input: ${result.onInvalidInputError.inputPath}"))
   408→                    }
   409→                    result?.onNotAuthenticatedError != null -> {
   410→                        Result.failure(Exception("Not authenticated"))
   411→                    }
   412→                    else -> Result.failure(Exception("Unknown error"))
   413→                }
   414→            }
   415→        } catch (e: Exception) {
   416→            Result.failure(e)
   417→        }
   418→    }

   725→    suspend fun deletePost(postId: String): Result<Unit> {
   726→        return try {
   727→            val response = apolloClient.mutation(DeletePostMutation(postId)).execute()
   728→            if (response.hasErrors()) {
   729→                Result.failure(Exception(response.errors?.firstOrNull()?.message ?: "Unknown error"))
   730→            } else {
   731→                val result = response.data?.deletePost
   732→                when {
   733→                    result?.onDeletePostPayload != null -> Result.success(Unit)
   734→                    result?.onInvalidInputError != null ->
   735→                        Result.failure(Exception("Invalid input: ${result.onInvalidInputError.inputPath}"))
   736→                    result?.onNotAuthenticatedError != null ->
   737→                        Result.failure(Exception("Not authenticated"))
   738→                    result?.onSharedPostDeletionNotAllowedError != null ->

   846→    private fun NotificationsQuery.Node.toNotification(): Notification? {
   847→        val actors = actors.edges.map { it.node.actorFields.toActor() }

   878→            onShareNotification != null -> Notification.Share(

   882→                actors = actors,

   885→            onReactNotification != null -> Notification.React(

   896→                    )
   897→                },
   898→                post = onReactNotification.post?.postFields?.toPost()
   899→            )
   900→            else -> null
   901→        }
   902→    }

   904→    private fun GqlPostVisibility?.toPostVisibility(): PostVisibility {
   905→        return when (this) {

   907→            GqlPostVisibility.UNLISTED -> PostVisibility.UNLISTED
   908→            GqlPostVisibility.FOLLOWERS -> PostVisibility.FOLLOWERS
   909→            GqlPostVisibility.DIRECT -> PostVisibility.DIRECT
   910→            GqlPostVisibility.NONE -> PostVisibility.NONE
   911→            else -> PostVisibility.PUBLIC
   912→        }
   913→    }

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android">

    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.INTERNET" />

    <application
        android:name=".HackersPubApplication"
        android:allowBackup="true"
        android:icon="@mipmap/ic_launcher"
        android:label="@string/app_name"
        android:roundIcon="@mipmap/ic_launcher_round"
        android:supportsRtl="true"
        android:theme="@style/Theme.HackersPub">
        <activity
            android:name=".MainActivity"
            android:exported="true"
            android:theme="@style/Theme.HackersPub">
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.MAIN" />
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.LAUNCHER" />
            </intent-filter>
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" />
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.DEFAULT" />
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.BROWSABLE" />
                <data
                    android:scheme="hackerspub"
                    android:host="verify" />
            </intent-filter>
        </activity>
    </application>

</manifest>

    10→import com.apollographql.apollo.network.okHttpClient
    11→import dagger.Module
    12→import dagger.Provides
    13→import dagger.hilt.InstallIn
    14→import dagger.hilt.android.qualifiers.ApplicationContext
    15→import dagger.hilt.components.SingletonComponent
    16→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.first
    17→import kotlinx.coroutines.runBlocking
    18→import okhttp3.Interceptor
    19→import okhttp3.OkHttpClient

    21→import javax.inject.Singleton
    22→
    23→private val Context.dataStore: DataStore<Preferences> by preferencesDataStore(name = "hackerspub_prefs")
    24→
    25→@Module
    26→@InstallIn(SingletonComponent::class)
    27→object AppModule {
    28→
    29→    @Provides
    30→    @Singleton
    31→    fun provideDataStore(@ApplicationContext context: Context): DataStore<Preferences> {
    32→        return context.dataStore
    33→    }
    34→
    35→    @Provides
    36→    @Singleton
    37→    fun provideSessionManager(dataStore: DataStore<Preferences>): SessionManager {
    38→        return SessionManager(dataStore)
    39→    }
    40→
    41→    @Provides
    42→    @Singleton
    43→    fun provideApolloClient(
    44→        @ApplicationContext context: Context,
    45→        sessionManager: SessionManager
    46→    ): ApolloClient {
    47→        val sqlNormalizedCacheFactory = SqlNormalizedCacheFactory(context, "apollo_cache.db")
    48→
    49→        val authInterceptor = Interceptor { chain ->
    50→            val token = runBlocking { sessionManager.sessionToken.first() }
    51→            val request = if (token != null) {
    52→                chain.request().newBuilder()
    53→                    .addHeader("Authorization", "Bearer $token")
    54→                    .build()
    55→            } else {
    56→                chain.request()
    57→            }
    58→            chain.proceed(request)
    59→        }
    60→
    61→        val okHttpClient = OkHttpClient.Builder()
    62→            .addInterceptor(authInterceptor)
    63→            .build()
    64→
    65→        return ApolloClient.Builder()
    66→            .serverUrl("https://hackers.pub/graphql")
    67→            .okHttpClient(okHttpClient)
    68→            .normalizedCache(sqlNormalizedCacheFactory)
    69→            .build()
    70→    }
    71→}

sealed class Notification {
    abstract val id: String
    abstract val uuid: String
    abstract val created: Instant
    abstract val actors: List<Actor>

    data class Follow(
        override val id: String,
        override val uuid: String,
        override val created: Instant,
        override val actors: List<Actor>
    ) : Notification()

    data class Mention(
        override val id: String,
        override val uuid: String,
        override val created: Instant,
        override val actors: List<Actor>,
        val post: Post?
    ) : Notification()

    data class Reply(
        override val id: String,
        override val uuid: String,
        override val created: Instant,
        override val actors: List<Actor>,
        val post: Post?
    ) : Notification()

    data class Quote(
        override val id: String,
        override val uuid: String,
        override val created: Instant,
        override val actors: List<Actor>,
        val post: Post?
    ) : Notification()

    data class Share(
        override val id: String,
        override val uuid: String,
        override val created: Instant,
        override val actors: List<Actor>,
        val post: Post?
    ) : Notification()

    data class React(
        override val id: String,
        override val uuid: String,
        override val created: Instant,
        override val actors: List<Actor>,
        val emoji: String?,
        val customEmoji: CustomEmoji?,
        val post: Post?
    ) : Notification()
}

1→package pub.hackers.android.data.local
     2→
     3→import androidx.datastore.core.DataStore
     4→import androidx.datastore.preferences.core.Preferences
     5→import androidx.datastore.preferences.core.edit
     6→import androidx.datastore.preferences.core.stringPreferencesKey
     7→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.Flow
     8→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.map
     9→import javax.inject.Inject
    10→import javax.inject.Singleton
    11→
    12→@Singleton
    13→class SessionManager @Inject constructor(
    14→    private val dataStore: DataStore<Preferences>
    15→) {
    16→    companion object {
    17→        private val SESSION_TOKEN_KEY = stringPreferencesKey("session_token")
    18→        private val USERNAME_KEY = stringPreferencesKey("username")
    19→        private val USER_ID_KEY = stringPreferencesKey("user_id")
    20→        private val USER_HANDLE_KEY = stringPreferencesKey("user_handle")
    21→        private val USER_NAME_KEY = stringPreferencesKey("user_name")
    22→        private val USER_AVATAR_KEY = stringPreferencesKey("user_avatar")
    23→    }
    24→
    25→    val sessionToken: Flow<String?> = dataStore.data.map { preferences ->
    26→        preferences[SESSION_TOKEN_KEY]
    27→    }
    28→
    29→    val isLoggedIn: Flow<Boolean> = dataStore.data.map { preferences ->
    30→        preferences[SESSION_TOKEN_KEY] != null
    31→    }
    32→
    33→    val username: Flow<String?> = dataStore.data.map { preferences ->
    34→        preferences[USERNAME_KEY]
    35→    }
    36→
    37→    val userId: Flow<String?> = dataStore.data.map { preferences ->
    38→        preferences[USER_ID_KEY]
    39→    }
    40→
    41→    val userHandle: Flow<String?> = dataStore.data.map { preferences ->
    42→        preferences[USER_HANDLE_KEY]
    43→    }
    44→
    45→    val userName: Flow<String?> = dataStore.data.map { preferences ->
    46→        preferences[USER_NAME_KEY]
    47→    }
    48→
    49→    val userAvatar: Flow<String?> = dataStore.data.map { preferences ->
    50→        preferences[USER_AVATAR_KEY]
    51→    }
    52→
    53→    suspend fun saveSession(
    54→        token: String,
    55→        userId: String,
    56→        username: String,
    57→        handle: String,
    58→        name: String,
    59→        avatarUrl: String
    60→    ) {
    61→        dataStore.edit { preferences ->
    62→            preferences[SESSION_TOKEN_KEY] = token
    63→            preferences[USER_ID_KEY] = userId
    64→            preferences[USERNAME_KEY] = username
    65→            preferences[USER_HANDLE_KEY] = handle
    66→            preferences[USER_NAME_KEY] = name
    67→            preferences[USER_AVATAR_KEY] = avatarUrl
    68→        }
    69→    }
    70→
    71→    suspend fun clearSession() {
    72→        dataStore.edit { preferences ->
    73→            preferences.remove(SESSION_TOKEN_KEY)
    74→            preferences.remove(USER_ID_KEY)
    75→            preferences.remove(USERNAME_KEY)
    76→            preferences.remove(USER_HANDLE_KEY)
    77→            preferences.remove(USER_NAME_KEY)
    78→            preferences.remove(USER_AVATAR_KEY)
    79→        }
    80→    }
    81→}
    82→

input.content:
# Notification Polling Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Add background notification polling via WorkManager so users receive Android system notifications and see an unread dot on the bottom nav.

**Architecture:** A `CoroutineWorker` polls the existing GraphQL `Notifications` query every 15 minutes. A singleton `NotificationStateManager` persists last-seen/last-polled IDs in DataStore and exposes a `Flow<Boolean>` for unread state. The UI collects this flow to drive the bottom nav dot.

**Tech Stack:** WorkManager 2.10, Hilt Work 1.2, DataStore Preferences, Apollo GraphQL, Jetpack Compose

**Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-25-notification-polling-design.md`

---

## File Structure

### New files
| File | Responsibility |
|------|---------------|
| `app/src/main/java/pub/hackers/android/data/local/NotificationStateManager.kt` | Persists last-seen/last-polled notification IDs, exposes `hasUnread: Flow<Boolean>` |
| `app/src/main/java/pub/hackers/android/data/worker/NotificationWorker.kt` | Background polling worker — fetches notifications, updates state, posts system notifications |

### Modified files
| File | Change |
|------|--------|

### Task 3: Create NotificationStateManager

**Files:**
- Create: `app/src/main/java/pub/hackers/android/data/local/NotificationStateManager.kt`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create the file**

```kotlin
package pub.hackers.android.data.local

import androidx.datastore.core.DataStore
import androidx.datastore.preferences.core.Preferences
import androidx.datastore.preferences.core.edit
import androidx.datastore.preferences.core.stringPreferencesKey
import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.Flow
import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.map
import javax.inject.Inject
import javax.inject.Singleton

@Singleton
class NotificationStateManager @Inject constructor(
    private val dataStore: DataStore<Preferences>
) {
    companion object {
        private val LAST_SEEN_NOTIFICATION_ID = stringPreferencesKey("last_seen_notification_id")
        private val LAST_POLLED_NOTIFICATION_ID = stringPreferencesKey("last_polled_notification_id")
    }

    val hasUnread: Flow<Boolean> = dataStore.data.map { prefs ->
        val lastSeen = prefs[LAST_SEEN_NOTIFICATION_ID]
        val lastPolled = prefs[LAST_POLLED_NOTIFICATION_ID]
        lastPolled != null && lastPolled != lastSeen
    }

    suspend fun updateLastPolledId(id: String) {
        dataStore.edit { prefs ->
            prefs[LAST_POLLED_NOTIFICATION_ID] = id
        }
    }

    suspend fun markAsSeen() {
        dataStore.edit { prefs ->
            val lastPolled = prefs[LAST_POLLED_NOTIFICATION_ID]
            if (lastPolled != null) {
                prefs[LAST_SEEN_NOTIFICATION_ID] = lastPolled
            }
        }
    }

    suspend fun clear() {
        dataStore.edit { prefs ->
            prefs.remove(LAST_SEEN_NOTIFICATION_ID)
            prefs.remove(LAST_POLLED_NOTIFICATION_ID)
        }
    }
}
```

package pub.hackers.android

import android.app.Application
import android.app.NotificationChannel
import android.app.NotificationManager
import androidx.hilt.work.HiltWorkerFactory
import androidx.work.Configuration
import dagger.hilt.android.HiltAndroidApp
import javax.inject.Inject

@HiltAndroidApp
class HackersPubApplication : Application(), Configuration.Provider {

    @Inject
    lateinit var workerFactory: HiltWorkerFactory

    override val workManagerConfiguration: Configuration
        get() = Configuration.Builder()
            .setWorkerFactory(workerFactory)
            .build()

    override fun onCreate() {
        super.onCreate()
        createNotificationChannel()
    }

    private fun createNotificationChannel() {
        val channel = NotificationChannel(
            "hackerspub_notifications",
            "Hackers' Pub",
            NotificationManager.IMPORTANCE_DEFAULT
        ).apply {
            description = "Notifications from Hackers' Pub"
        }
        val notificationManager = getSystemService(NotificationManager::class.java)
        notificationManager.createNotificationChannel(channel)
    }
}
